nodes.title: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phr...
nodes.description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
nodes.subdomain: Language Standards

academic_standards.description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.

nodes.description: Gather relevant information from multiple authoritative print and digital sources, using advanced searches effectively; assess the usefulness of each source in answering the research question; integrate information into the text selectively to maintain the flow of ideas, avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.

nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary describing political, social, or economic aspects of history/social studies.

nodes.title: Analyze in detail a series of events described in a text
nodes.description: Analyze in detail a series of events described in a text; determine whether earlier events caused later ones or simply preceded them.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Analyze in detail a series of events described in a text; determine whether earlier events caused later ones or simply preceded them.

nodes.title: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source
nodes.description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of how key events or ideas develop over the course of the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of how key events or ideas develop over the course of the text.

nodes.title: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary s...
nodes.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, attending to such features as the date and origin of the information.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literacy in History/Social Studies 6—12

academic_standards.description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, attending to such features as the date and origin of the information.

nodes.title: By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades ...
nodes.description:
By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 9—10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description:
By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 9—10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literary nonfiction at the high end of the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

nodes.title: Analyze seminal U
nodes.description: Analyze seminal U.S. documents of historical and literary significance (e.g., Washington's Farewell Address, the Gettysburg Address, Roosevelt's Four Freedoms speech, King's "Letter from Birmingham Jail"), including how they address related themes and concepts.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze seminal U.S. documents of historical and literary significance (e.g., Washington's Farewell Address, the Gettysburg Address, Roosevelt's Four Freedoms speech, King's "Letter from Birmingham Jail"), including how they address related themes and concepts.

nodes.title: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing ...
nodes.description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning.

nodes.title: Analyze various accounts of a subject told in different mediums (e
nodes.description: Analyze various accounts of a subject told in different mediums (e.g., a person's life story in both print and multimedia), determining which details are emphasized in each account.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze various accounts of a subject told in different mediums (e.g., a person's life story in both print and multimedia), determining which details are emphasized in each account.

nodes.title: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how an a...
nodes.description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how an author uses rhetoric to advance that point of view or purpose.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how an author uses rhetoric to advance that point of view or purpose.

nodes.title: Analyze in detail how an author's ideas or claims are developed and refined b...
nodes.description: Analyze in detail how an author's ideas or claims are developed and refined by particular sentences, paragraphs, or larger portions of a text (e.g., a section or chapter).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze in detail how an author's ideas or claims are developed and refined by particular sentences, paragraphs, or larger portions of a text (e.g., a section or chapter).

nodes.title: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, includ...
nodes.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language of a court opinion differs from that of a newspaper).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language of a court opinion differs from that of a newspaper).

nodes.title: Analyze how the author unfolds an analysis or series of ideas or events, incl...
nodes.description: Analyze how the author unfolds an analysis or series of ideas or events, including the order in which the points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections that are drawn between them.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Analyze how the author unfolds an analysis or series of ideas or events, including the order in which the points are made, how they are introduced and developed, and the connections that are drawn between them.

nodes.title: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the cours...
nodes.description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.

nodes.title: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the tex...
nodes.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Informational Text

academic_standards.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

nodes.title: By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dra...
nodes.description:
By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 9—10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description:
By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 9—10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.

nodes.title: Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific w...
nodes.description: Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific work (e.g., how Shakespeare treats a theme or topic from Ovid or the Bible or how a later author draws on a play by Shakespeare).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze how an author draws on and transforms source material in a specific work (e.g., how Shakespeare treats a theme or topic from Ovid or the Bible or how a later author draws on a play by Shakespeare).

nodes.title: Analyze the representation of a subject or a key scene in two different artis...
nodes.description: Analyze the representation of a subject or a key scene in two different artistic mediums, including what is emphasized or absent in each treatment (e.g., Auden's "Musée des Beaux Arts" and Breughel's Landscape with the Fall of Icarus).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze the representation of a subject or a key scene in two different artistic mediums, including what is emphasized or absent in each treatment (e.g., Auden's "Musée des Beaux Arts" and Breughel's Landscape with the Fall of Icarus).

nodes.title: Analyze a particular point of view or cultural experience reflected in a work...
nodes.description: Analyze a particular point of view or cultural experience reflected in a work of literature from outside the United States, drawing on a wide reading of world literature.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze a particular point of view or cultural experience reflected in a work of literature from outside the United States, drawing on a wide reading of world literature.

nodes.title: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure a text, order eve...
nodes.description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure a text, order events within it (e.g., parallel plots), and manipulate time (e.g., pacing, flashbacks) create such effects as mystery, tension, or surprise.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure a text, order events within it (e.g., parallel plots), and manipulate time (e.g., pacing, flashbacks) create such effects as mystery, tension, or surprise.

nodes.title: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, incl...
nodes.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language evokes a sense of time and place; how it sets a formal or informal tone).
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language evokes a sense of time and place; how it sets a formal or informal tone).

nodes.title: Analyze how complex characters (e
nodes.description: Analyze how complex characters (e.g., those with multiple or conflicting motivations) develop over the course of a text, interact with other characters, and advance the plot or develop the theme.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

academic_standards.description: Analyze how complex characters (e.g., those with multiple or conflicting motivations) develop over the course of a text, interact with other characters, and advance the plot or develop the theme.

nodes.title: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze in detail its develop...
nodes.description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze in detail its development over the course of the text, including how it emerges and is shaped and refined by specific details; provide an objective summary of the text.

nodes.description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
nodes.subdomain: Reading Standards for Literature

nodes.subdomain: College and Career Readiness Anchor Standards for Reading

nodes.title: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to bu...
nodes.description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.

nodes.title: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining...
